sanskrit: यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्युषितं च यत् । उच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम् ॥
hindiTranslation: जो भोजन अधपका, रसरहित, दुर्गन्धित, बासी और उच्छिष्ट है तथा जो महान् अपवित्र भी है, वह तामस मनुष्यको प्रिय होता है ॥
englishTranslation: Food that is half-cooked, tasteless, foul-smelling, stale, leftover, and impure is dear to people ruled by tamas.
essence: What you repeatedly choose to consume reveals the heaviness within.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: People dominated by tamas are drawn to food that is stale, tasteless, foul-smelling, leftover, or impure.
simpleInsight: Your cravings can reveal your condition before your thoughts do.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Krishna is teaching Arjuna how faith shows itself in daily choices. After describing pure and restless habits, he names the food preferred by tamas: stale, impure, and lifeless. The point is to show how inner heaviness shapes appetite.
modernRelevance: You open the fridge late at night and reach for whatever is dead easiest, not what actually helps you. The body keeps a record of what the mind has been feeding it.
translationLiteral: यातयामम् (half-cooked / stale) / गतरसम् (devoid of taste) / पूति (foul-smelling) / पर्युषितम् (stale / left overnight) / च (and) / यत् (which) / उच्छिष्टम् (leftover) / अपि (even) / च (and) / अमेध्यम् (impure) / भोजनम् (food) / तामसप्रियम् (dear to tamasic people)
